diagrams.title: File System On-Disk Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the typical layout of a disk partition with file system components
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where different file system components are stored on disk and their roles in file management

diagrams.title: Inode Structure
diagrams.description: Internal structure of a UNIX inode showing metadata fields and block pointers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how file metadata and data block addresses are stored, enabling both direct and indirect block addressing

diagrams.title: File Allocation Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of contiguous, linked, and indexed allocation
diagrams.purpose: Comparing how different allocation methods store file data blocks and their implications for access speed and fragmentation

diagrams.title: Hierarchical Directory Structure
diagrams.description: Tree diagram showing root directory with subdirectories and files
diagrams.purpose: Understanding directory organization and file path navigation in modern operating systems
